## Quick orientation for AI coding agents

This repo implements solute transport in fractured porous media in C++.
Below are the minimal facts an AI needs to be immediately productive and safe edits to this codebase.

### Big picture (what runs and why)
- Entry point: `PERFORM.cpp` — reads parameters, builds the `Domain` and `NetworkMeshes`, optionally computes a backbone, runs `FlowComputation`, then constructs a `Transport` object which runs particle transport and writes `Results`.
- Major components (directories):
  - `Domain_Definition/` — fracture & mesh data structures (`FractureMesh.h`, `NetworkMeshes.h`, `Domain.h`) and flow/velocity utilities.
  - `Transport/` — particle transport logic (`Transport.h`, `Particle.cpp`) and transfer-time models.
  - `Flow/` — flow solver interfaces (`FlowComputation.h`) used by `NetworkMeshes::FlowComputation`.
  - `Chemistry/` — chemistry routines that can modify fracture apertures; `NetworkMeshes` includes `Chemistry.h` and produces `net_mesh_modified`.
  - `Input_Output/` — `Parameters.h`, boundary conditions and `Results` writing.
  - `Utilitaries/` — numeric helpers, RNG (`rngstream`/`RandomNumber.h`), CGAL types, and custom containers.

### Key data flows & coupling points
- `PERFORM.cpp` orchestrates the simulation sequence; changes to control flow or simulation options should start here.
- `NetworkMeshes` generates or reads the DFN, computes velocities, and exposes `return_backbone`, `EvaluateFlowVelocities`, and `return_ave_vel`.
- `Transport` depends on `NetworkMeshes`, `Domain`, and `Parameters`. It stores `net_mesh_modified` (aperture-altered meshes) to persist chemistry effects.
- Chemistry hooks: `NetworkMeshes` includes `Chemistry.h` and has functions like `ChangeAperture` and `print_DFN_in_file_aperture`. Editing reactive updates should consider both `NetworkMeshes` and `Transport::net_mesh_modified`.

### Project-specific conventions and patterns
- Many shared typedefs and containers live in `Input_Output/Parameters.h` (e.g., `MapSegmentTimes`, `MapSegmentVelocities`) — prefer these when adding map-like data.
- Geometric primitives use CGAL wrapper types (`CgalPoint2D`, `Segment2D`, pointcpp<T>), and orthogonal projection utilities are in `Transport` and `Utilitaries/Point_Cgal.*`.
- Randomness: RNG is centralized via `RngStream_a` / `rngstream` and passed into `Transport` (do not replace with local rand() without intent).
- Indexing: `NetworkMeshes` and `FractureMesh` use integer indices for meshes, nodes and fractures — keep map keys consistent (int-based) to avoid subtle serialization/indexing bugs.

### Build & run (what I found and assumptions)
- I did not find a Makefile or CMake config in the repository root. If you have a CI/build script outside `src/`, supply it; otherwise the project appears to be built manually.
- Assumption (confirm with maintainer): the project requires Boost and CGAL. A conservative example compile line (example only — confirm flags and libs):

```bash
# Example (assumption) — adapt include/library paths and C++ standard as needed
g++ -std=c++11 -O2 src/*.cpp src/*/*.cpp -I/path/to/boost -I/path/to/CGAL \
    -lCGAL -lgmp -lmpfr -lboost_system -lboost_filesystem -o perform
```

Ask the maintainer for the canonical build command or provide the repo root build files.

### Where to look for concrete examples when editing
- To change entry/control flow: `PERFORM.cpp`.
- To add/modify transport logic: `Transport/Transport.h` and `Transport/Transport.cpp` (implementation files in the `Transport/` directory).
- To inspect geometry & mesh behavior: `Domain_Definition/FractureMesh.h` and `NetworkMeshes.h`.
- For parameters and I/O shapes: `Input_Output/Parameters.h`, `BoundaryConditions.h`, and `Results.cpp`.

### Safe-edit rules for AI agents (do not change these without human sign-off)
- Do not change RNG seeding and `RngStream_a` usage silently; it affects reproducibility across simulations.
- When editing numeric algorithms (advection/diffusion solvers), preserve existing method signatures — many functions are used in maps or passed-by-reference across modules.
- When altering file formats produced by `print_DFN_in_file*` or `Results::writing`, update all readers/writers (visualization tools expect stable text formats).

### If you need more info
- Provide the project's canonical build command or CI config if available.
- Tell me which workflows you expect me to modify (DFN generation, flow solver, transport kernels, chemistry coupling) and I will update these instructions with code examples and targeted editing guidelines.
